inter_AIS.note: the guard delegate; processOrder's behavior depends on it
the terminal delegate in the sequence

rationale: `processOrder` is a facade sequencing three delegates (validate, enrich, persist) with one guard. Its true blast radius is the delegation chain — the inter_AIS — not its own body, which only routes values between calls. intra_AIS is empty: the guard return and the local `enriched` binding carry no independent work beyond shuttling delegate results. PDG mode (intra-procedural) has ~0 inter-AIS recall here BY DESIGN; call-graph mode walks validate->enrich->persist. Anchors the `inter` stratum with a sequential-delegation shape (distinct from the dispatcher's branching shape). The single guard gives `processOrder` a CDG edge so the smoke test's edge-presence requirement is met.
